title: כרתים
excerpt: מדריך כרתים לישראלים: בחירת בסיס בין חניה, רתימנו והרקליון, טיסות לשני שדות, רכב ותחבורה, קנוסוס וסמריה, חופים, כשרות, תקציב וכללי כניסה, עם מקורות רשמיים ותאריך בדיקה.
author: מערכת Tra-Vel
reviewMethod: Prioritize official Greek tourism, regional government, airport, public transport, culture-ministry ticketing, national park, port authority and Israeli consular sources. Recheck fares, timetables, opening seasons, entry rules, kosher availability and security guidance immediately before publication.
bestFor: 5 עד 10 ימים עם בסיס אחד או שניים
sections: Seasons, meltemi wind and heat planning

facts.claim: The Greek National Tourism Organisation presents Crete by regions, with Chania and Rethymno in the west, Heraklion as the largest urban centre and Lasithi in the east.
Incredible Crete is the official tourism site of the Region of Crete covering the island's four regional units.
Chania International Airport is operated by Fraport Greece and publishes direct bus and taxi guidance for reaching the city.
Heraklion Urban Bus operates the city network including the airport connection, with published itineraries and tickets.
KTEL Heraklion Lasithi operates daily year-round intercity routes from Heraklion toward Agios Nikolaos, Ierapetra and Sitia plus an express north-coast service toward Chania.
Hellenic Heritage is the official Ministry of Culture and ODAP ticket service for Knossos and other Crete sites, listing the full Knossos ticket at 20 euros when checked on 2026-07-21.
The Heraklion Archaeological Museum listed full admission at 20 euros and reduced at 12 euros with long daily opening hours when checked on 2026-07-21.
Samaria National Park is managed under NECCA and publishes official notices, opening information and e-tickets; the gorge operates in a defined season subject to weather closures.
The Heraklion Port Authority operates one of Greece's major passenger ports, serving coastal shipping and cruise traffic that connects Crete with Piraeus and the islands.
Etz Hayyim in Chania is the only active synagogue in Crete today and welcomes visitors for tours and services.
Chabad of Crete operates in Hersonissos and is the contact point for Shabbat meals and kosher arrangements on the island, with seasonal availability.
The Israeli embassy in Athens states that Israeli passport holders do not need a visa for tourist visits to Greece, subject to Schengen stay limits and passport validity requirements.

The European Commission states that the Entry/Exit System began progressive rollout on 2025-10-12 and became fully operational on 2026-04-10, registering non-EU nationals biometrically at external Schengen borders.
Israeli travelers should verify Greece in the National Security Council travel-warning system immediately before departure.
